summaryText: Guest Pastor Leo Bigger taught on the theme 'Reframe It,' encouraging believers to shift their perspective during difficult seasons. He began by sharing the story of Alfred Nobel, who reframed his legacy from 'merchant of death' to founder of the Nobel Prize, illustrating that we can rewrite our story with God's help. Pastor Leo then turned to the life of Jacob, who faced betrayal and disappointment from his father-in-law Laban. From Jacob's story, we learn to proclaim God's future over our circumstances, to visualize and focus on God's vision for our lives, and to never give up because God is always in our story. The message emphasized the power of spoken words to bring heaven's reality into our earthly situations, as demonstrated when God spoke creation into existence. Pastor Leo shared personal and modern testimonies, including a church member who sold his house and the founder of IT Cosmetics, to show that what we consistently speak and visualize, we become. He closed with prayer, asking the Holy Spirit to give everyone a godly vision for their future, framing not our pain (Benoni) but our blessing (Benjamin).
mainPoints: Proclaim God's future over your life; speak blessings and double restoration into your circumstances.
Let a godly vision drive your destiny; consistently visualize and focus on what God says about you.
Never give up; however difficult the situation, God is in your story and will bring good from it.

versesMentioned.text: Let me go through all your flocks today and remove from them every speckled or spotted sheep, every dark-colored lamb and every spotted or speckled goat. They will be my wages.

Then he placed the peeled branches in all the watering troughs, so that they would be directly in front of the flocks when they came to drink. When the flocks were in heat and came to drink, they mated in front of the branches. And they bore young that were streaked or speckled or spotted.

Monday.scripture: Genesis 1:3 - And God said, "Let there be light," and there was light.
Monday.focus: The power of God's spoken word.
Monday.action: Speak one promise from God over your day out loud.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help my words today create life and reflect Your truth.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 18:21 - The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
Tuesday.action: Intentionally bless someone with your words today, either in person or in a message.
Tuesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guard my mouth. Let my words bring life and encouragement.

Wednesday.scripture: Habakkuk 2:2 - Then the LORD replied: "Write down the revelation and make it plain on tablets so that a herald may run with it."
Wednesday.action: Write down or draw one thing you believe God has in store for your future.
Wednesday.prayer: God, give me a clear vision of the 'Benjamin' future You have for me, not the 'Benoni' pain.

Thursday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 4:18 - So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, since what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal.
Thursday.action: Place a visual reminder (a note, a picture) of God's promise where you will see it throughout the day.
Thursday.prayer: Father, help me to see my situation from Your heavenly perspective today.

Friday.scripture: Galatians 6:9 - Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
Friday.action: Identify one area where you feel like quitting. Commit to one small, faithful step forward in it.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, strengthen me to endure. Remind me that You are in my story and Your timing is perfect.

Saturday.scripture: Genesis 50:20 - You intended to harm me, but God intended it for good to accomplish what is now being done, the saving of many lives.
Saturday.action: Reflect on a past disappointment. Write down one way God could use it for good or has already begun to.
Saturday.prayer: God, I trust that You can reframe every hurt for Your glory. Turn my mourning into dancing.
